cards.back: Cloud-native reverse proxy and ingress controller — auto-discovers services, handles TLS, routing, middleware
Traefik: dynamic config, auto-discovers K8s services, built-in Let's Encrypt, middleware CRDs. nginx: more mature, higher performance at scale
Network entry point — defines port and protocol. web (80), websecure (443) are standard. Configured at Traefik startup
Matches incoming requests (by host, path, headers) and routes to a service. Defined via Ingress annotations or IngressRoute CRD

cards.front: What is a Service in Traefik?
cards.back: Load balancer that forwards traffic to backend pods — wraps K8s Service. Supports weighted round-robin, sticky sessions
Transforms requests/responses between router and service — auth, redirects, rate limiting, headers, compression
Traefik-native routing config — more powerful than standard Ingress. Supports TCP, UDP, middleware chains, priority
Built-in ACME client — annotation: traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.tls.certresolver: letsencrypt. Auto-issues and renews certs
Traefik forwards TLS without terminating — backend handles TLS. Used when backend requires end-to-end encryption (e.g. ArgoCD gRPC)

cards.front: HTTP to HTTPS redirect in Traefik?
cards.back: Use a Middleware with redirectScheme: https, then apply to the web (port 80) entrypoint router
Annotation to enable TLS on a router — "true" enables TLS termination at Traefik
Protects routes with HTTP basic auth — references a K8s Secret with htpasswd-encoded credentials
traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: namespace-middlewarename@kubernetescrd
Redirects HTTP to HTTPS: spec.redirectScheme.scheme: https, permanent: true
Removes path prefix before forwarding — e.g. /app/foo → /foo. Useful for path-based routing
Limits requests per second per source IP — spec.rateLimit.average, burst
k3s ships Traefik v2/v3 as the default ingress controller — pre-installed, no setup needed
Disabled by default. Enable via HelmChartConfig or port-forward: kubectl port-forward -n kube-system svc/traefik 9000:9000
nip.io resolves IP-based hostnames (argocd.1.2.3.4.nip.io → 1.2.3.4) — no DNS setup needed for dev/demo clusters
Higher priority routes match first — annotation: traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.priority: "100". Default priority = rule length
